reflexion_entries_to_archive: S121-P1 #telegraph-cross-peer-by-derivation: the physics conferral (bond.stiffnessMultiplier) is host-only + un-synced, so a joiner cannot read it. Deriving the STRUCTURAL relation (un-fouled hub + magic neighbor sharing an endpoint) render-side from synced graph state + world.tick gives a bit-identical cross-peer telegraph with ZERO wire bytes. General pattern for visualizing an ephemeral host quantity: recompute from synced inputs, never sync the derived value.

S121-P2 #income-keystone-replay-self-consistent-not-byte-identical: unlike the S118 rigidity Keystone (ephemeral stiffnessMultiplier -> byte-identical), the income keystone changes SCORE values BY DESIGN. It is still safe because save.replay asserts run-A==run-B (self-consistency), NOT golden fixtures -> both runs use the new formula and match. The gate is (a) mirror the term into the differential-test INDEPENDENT oracle (else the equivalence test fails) + (b) update any pre-existing test that hardcoded a now-changed value. Check whether replay tests are self-consistency vs golden BEFORE assuming a scoring change needs fixture regen.

S121 #council-plan-value-check-value-refute: PLAN-phase Council caught a REAL balance finding (magic-bond term is uncapped scoring.ts:189 -> income keystone amplifies clustering) -> adopted per-Filament cap. But BOTH seats' Q3 determinism criticals (non-dyadic 0.3 -> cross-arch FP desync; sort-by-id tiebreaker) were REFUTED on merits (JS doubles are spec-deterministic; existing x0.05 non-dyadic passes byte-identical replay; a pure count has no tiebreaker). 10th #empirical-refutes-plausible-criticals: verify determinism criticals against language semantics + the existing passing suite before conceding.

S121-P4 #copy-budgets-beat-fit-guards: the text-overflow class dies at AUTHORING time, not render time — put char budgets on the copy source (tested: name<=16/power<=34/recipe<=150) and keep fitText guards only as the safety net. The 34-char power budget came from a LIVE bounds measurement (42 chars escaped a 240px tile even at the fontSize floor) — measure the real zone before writing the budget, then encode it as a test so future copy fails in vitest instead of on screen.

S121-P4 #placeholder-art-is-a-liability: three towers shipped sessions ago with a voltkin-zap placeholder sprite and it silently became the owner-visible 'voltkin everywhere' bug. When a real asset is missing, prefer a PROCEDURAL representation derived from game truth (the recipe constellation in the board's own glyph language) over borrowing another entry's art — it can never be wrong, never drifts, and costs zero assets. Encode the coherence rule as a test (sprite XOR emblem + anti-placeholder regression).
